title: Heptapleurum gracilipes
taxonomy: Heptapleurum gracilipes is classified in the family Araliaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the ivy family. It belongs to the genus Heptapleurum, which is a group of climbing plants native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The Araliaceae family is part of the order Apiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Apiales order. Heptapleurum gracilipes is a unique species within this classification, with distinct features that set it apart from other plants in the Araliaceae family.

intro: Heptapleurum gracilipes, also known as the umbrella fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum gracilipes include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
